case_title: ALIPHCOM, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ELIZABETH BASTIAANSE HAMREN et al

docket_entries.description: SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT AND COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF ALIPHCOM, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION SERVED JUL-02-2009, MAIL AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT, ACKNOWLEDGMENT DATE JUL-21-2009 AS TO DEFENDANT HAMREN, ELIZABETH BASTIAANSE ,AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM, DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SEXUAL HARASSMENT TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING SEVERE OR PERVASIVE LEVEL. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. STOCK OPTIONS ARE NOT WAGES (INTERNATIONAL BUSINESS MACHINES CORP. VS. BAJOREK (9TH CIR. 1999) 191 F.3D 1033, 1039-40.) MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ORDER ON ALIPHCOM'S DEMURRERS [OVERRULED IN PART & SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART] AND MOTION TO STRIKE [DENIED] PORTIONS OF HAMREN'S CROSS-COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, HOSTILE WORK ENVIRONMENT/SEXUAL HARASSMENT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST PLEAD FACTS DEMONSTRATING SEVERE OR PERVASIVE WORKPLACE HARASSMENT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, WRONGFUL TERMINATION. STOCK OPTIONS ARE NOT WAGES OR EARNED COMPENSATION. INTERNATIONAL BUSINESS MACHINES CORP. VS. BAJOREK (9TH CIR.) (1999) 191 F.3D 1033, 1039-1040. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

(Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plantronics To Produce Documents
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM'S (AMENDED) MOTION TO COMPEL PLANTRONICS TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS.

APPEARANCES: Lowell D. Mead, Esq. of Cooley LLP 693-2146 for pltf/x-deft & moving party; Jonathan Lange, Esq. of Irell & Manella LLP 310/277-1010 for nonparty Plantronics, opposing.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Plaintiff's requests are overbroad and burdensome. (See Calcor Space Facility Inc. v. Superior Court of Orange County (1997) 53 Cal.App. 4th 216). Aliphcom may review the documents that Plantronics has provided, then propound more narrowly tailored and calculated requests if necessary. (302/CCW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Section 2019.210 disclosure statement. The disclosure statement shall be produced to Plantronic's counsel subject to the Confidentiality Order entered by this court on December 22, 2009. Off Calendar Mar-05-2010 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: David A. Lowe, Esq. of Rudy Exelrod Zieff & Lowe LLP (415) 434-9800, appearing for defendant and cross-complainant Elizabeth Hamren & opposing party; Richard H. Frank, of Cooley Godward Kronish LLP (415) 693-2043 appearing for moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to second cause of action, hostile work environment/sexual harassment. Cross-complainant must plead facts demonstrating severe or pervasive workplace harassment. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to fifth cause of action, wrongful termination. Stock options are not wages or earned compensation. International Business Machines Corp. vs. Bajorek (9th Cir.) (1999) 191 F.3d 1033, 1039-1040. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM, DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINT. Appearances: David A. Lowe, Esq. of Rudy Exelrod Zieff & Lowe LLP (415) 434-9800, appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Elizabeth Bastiaanse Hamren; Richard H. Frank of Cooley Godward Kronish LLP (415) 693-2138, appearing for Plaintiff and cross Defendant Aliphcom. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to second cause of action for sexual harassment to allege facts supporting severe or pervasive level. Demurrer overruled as to third cause of action for retaliation. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to fifth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Stock options are not wages (International Business Machines Corp. vs. Bajorek (9th Cir. 1999) 191 F.3d 1033, 1039-40.) Motion to strike is denied. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
